domain: Lakota Winter Count (Waniyetu Wówapi), Plains Indians 18th c.+: pictographic year-record system; 1 most-defining event per year as single pictograph; year-glyphs arranged in spiral on hide; transmitted by appointed keeper (eldest male of band) to apprentice; multiple bands' winter counts compared for canonical-event consensus.
specific_mechanism: WANIYETU-WOWAPI-PER-YEAR-GLYPH-SELECTION-SPIRAL-KEEPER-LINEAGE-CROSS-BAND-CONSENSUS-MEMORY: LLM memory-architecture with (1) N_year 1-glyph-per-year pictographic chain: one canonical-event-representation per memory-time-unit; (2) E_criterion canonical-event selection filter: most-defining event criterion; (3) L_spiral codex-layout per-band geographic-keeper: spiral writing + band-locality structure; (4) K_inherit intergenerational-keeper transmission: oldest-keeper lineage propagation of N_year chain; (5) C_consensus cross-band canonical-event-name agreement: multi-band reconciliation of year-glyph naming.
llm_application: WANIYETU-WOWAPI LLM mechanism + N_year + E_criterion + L_spiral + K_inherit + C_consensus. Differs from R504. R517. R529. R542. R554. R567 (closest — 6+6 active-dormant cycle + 3-anchor calibration + 8+1 day private-public + 4-month intermediate + dormant mountain store). Differs from MemAgents + AgentMemSurvey + H-MEM + Episodic + MemEval by 1-glyph-per-year + E_criterion filter + L_spiral keeper + K_inherit lineage + C_consensus.
novelty_claim: MemAgents + AgentMemSurvey + H-MEM + Episodic + MemEval cover memory + episodic + hierarchical + agentic paradigm. WANIYETU adds (a) N_year + (b) E_criterion + (c) L_spiral keeper + (d) K_inherit lineage + (e) C_consensus. K_sub_mechanisms=5.
